mission.title: flux: Feature Request: Allow `flux diff ks` when the `Kustomization` does not already exist
mission.description: Feature Request: Allow `flux diff ks` when the `Kustomization` does not already exist. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current flux deployment
steps.description:
Verify your flux version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
helm list -n flux 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working flux installation.

steps.title: Review flux configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get configmap -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flux
```
Related: #3270

`flux diff` is great for identifying changes when the `Kustomization` object already exists on the cluster.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Feature Request: Allow `flux diff ks` when the…
steps.description:
One half of changes necessary to resolve https://github.com/fluxcd/flux2/issues/5781 ; makes use of changes introduced in the `pkg` dependency by https://github.com/fluxcd/pkg/pull/1153

As suggested, this change keeps the current behavior for building kustomizations internally as part of `flux build kustomization ...`, but allows users to opt-in with the `--in-memory-build` flag, which will use `filesys.MakeFsInMemory()`. 

To keep things a bit cleaner, I opted to add a filesystem "backend"

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get events -n flux --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Feature Request: Allow `flux diff ks` when the…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
One half of changes necessary to resolve https://github.com/fluxcd/flux2/issues/5781 ; makes use of changes introduced in the `pkg` dependency by https://github.com/fluxcd/pkg/pull/1153

As suggested, this change keeps the current behavior for building kustomizations internally as part of `flux build kustomization ...`, but allows users to opt-in with the `--in-memory-build` flag, which will use

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible.
